meta.name: SBQ-R Suicidal Behaviors Questionnaire
meta.description: The SBQ-R is a 4-item self-report measure of suicidality. It assesses four dimensions: lifetime suicidal ideation and/or suicide attempt, frequency of suicidal ideation over the past 12 months, threat of suicide attempt, and self-reported likelihood of suicidal behavior in the future. It is widely used for screening in both clinical and research settings.
meta.author: Osman et al. (2001)
meta.reference: Osman A, Bagge CL, Gutierrez PM, Konick LC, Kopper BA, Barrios FX. The Suicidal Behaviors Questionnaire-Revised (SBQ-R): Validation with clinical and nonclinical samples. Assessment. 2001;8(4):443-454.
meta.instruction: Please answer each question carefully. Your honest responses are important. If you are currently experiencing suicidal thoughts, please contact a crisis helpline or seek immediate professional help.

questions.text: Have you ever thought about or attempted to kill yourself?

choices.label: It was just a brief passing thought
I have had a plan at least once to kill myself but did not try to do it
I have attempted to kill myself, but did not want to die

questions.text: How often have you thought about killing yourself in the past year?
Have you ever told someone that you were going to commit suicide, or that you might do it?

choices.label: Yes, at one time, but did not really want to die
Yes, more than once, but did not want to do it

questions.text: How likely is it that you will attempt suicide someday?

ranges.label: Low Risk
ranges.description: Your responses indicate a low level of suicidal risk. However, any suicidal thoughts should be taken seriously. This indicates that your mood has been relatively stable recently, with no significant signs of persistent sadness or loss of interest.
suggestions: If you ever experience distressing thoughts, do not hesitate to reach out for help
Maintain healthy social connections and coping strategies
Know the contact information for crisis helplines in your area
Maintain regular social activities and hobbies
Set aside time each day for activities you enjoy

ranges.label: Moderate Risk
ranges.description: Your score of 7 or above is at the clinical cutoff and indicates a significant level of suicidal risk. Further professional evaluation is strongly recommended.
suggestions: Please seek a professional mental health evaluation as soon as possible
Contact a crisis helpline if you are in immediate distress
Talk to a trusted person about how you are feeling
Develop a safety plan with a mental health professional
Consider consulting a mental health counselor for professional guidance

ranges.label: High Risk
ranges.description: Your responses indicate a high level of suicidal risk. Immediate professional intervention is recommended. Your score suggests notable emotional distress that may be significantly affecting your quality of life. Reaching out to a mental health professional is recommended.
suggestions: Please contact a crisis helpline or go to your nearest emergency room immediately
Do not leave yourself alone if you are having active suicidal thoughts
Remove access to any means of self-harm
Reach out to a mental health professional urgently
Schedule an appointment with a mental health professional as soon as possible

ranges.label: Very High Risk
ranges.description: Your responses indicate a very high level of suicidal risk. Immediate crisis intervention is essential. Your score suggests notable emotional distress that may be significantly affecting your quality of life. Reaching out to a mental health professional is recommended.
suggestions: Please call emergency services (e.g., 988 Suicide & Crisis Lifeline) or go to your nearest emergency room NOW
Do not be alone — stay with a trusted person
Remove all access to means of self-harm immediately
This is a crisis situation — professional help is needed right away
Schedule an appointment with a mental health professional as soon as possible
